purpose: machine-readable lightweight gap phase registry that turns broad goals into bounded phase queues without adding durable process logs
machineBoundary: .runtime phase run artifacts are temporary; durable truth stays in contracts, registered tests, source, and sanitized foldback summaries

phases.objective: Claim UI/UX v1 production acceptance only after human owner receipt and sanitized production evidence exist, without claiming a complete design system.
entryCriteria: repo-local responsive visual QA evidence is folded into web GUI product contract
Figma MCP source context remains pinned
human owner accepted the current production v1 surface claim
exitCriteria: production browser e2e or production screenshot evidence is folded back as sanitized summary
repo-local accessibility closeout boundary remains folded back without claiming assistive technology conformance
complete design system remains explicitly not claimed
acceptance: owner receipt accepted ui_ux_v1_production_accepted for the current production v1 surface
production UI evidence is folded back as a sanitized summary, not raw logs or screenshots in git
repo-local accessibility closeout may claim contrast, keyboard path, and API key modal focus trap browser checks only; full accessibility still requires assistive technology review
complete UI/UX design system remains cannot-claim
nextStepOpeners: Open complete design system work only after a separate owner, consumer, contract, and eval are approved
Keep complete design system and production visual polish out of the current claim

ownerReceipt.acceptedScope: current production UI/UX v1 product surface only

proves: UI/UX v1 production acceptance claim is gated by owner receipt and sanitized production evidence
doesNotProve: production visual polish complete without owner receipt

phases.objective: Confirm readonly projection with secret-gated production dogfood and fold back only sanitized evidence.
acceptance: readonly foldback policy requires OPL_PRODUCTION_DOGFOOD_MEDOPL_READONLY=1
dogfood stdout confirms runtime status, materials/deliverables, and billing readonly projection checks
release evidence foldback stores sanitized summary only and forbids raw logs or secret values
production readonly switch and sanitized stdout evidence are folded back for run 28039468173
nextStepOpeners: Keep future readonly evidence folded back through release:evidence with sanitized summaries only
Do not infer MedOPL runtime execution, payment readiness, or long-term stability from readonly projection dogfood
Reopen production_readonly_foldback only if a future rollout regresses readonly projection coverage

ownerReceipt.reason: the blocker is missing production evidence, not missing product owner approval

phases.objective: Accept the current runtime execution boundary as fail-closed with an empty allowlist; Web remains gate plus readonly projection only.
entryCriteria: Go control plane remains only backend business entry
exitCriteria: MedOPL and OPL Framework remain execution authority
acceptance: executionAdmission currentStatus stays not_admitted
current allowlist is empty and Web may not install, repair, module exec, return artifact body, or mutate runtime
MedOPL and OPL Framework remain execution authority while Web remains gate plus readonly projection
future runtime execution still requires Go-side contract, registered eval, explicit command allowlist, human authorization, tenant audit, and artifact/body authority contract
nextStepOpeners: Keep runtime execution disabled unless a future owner explicitly opens runtime_execution_contract_design
If future execution is requested, add Go-side contract, allowlist eval, and authorization boundary before any Web runtime mutation route
Continue to list OPL runtime execution from Web and artifact body authority as cannot-claim

ownerReceipt.reason: owner accepted the current fail-closed runtime boundary rather than admitting execution

proves: runtime execution admission is either fully satisfied or fail-closed

phases.objective: Keep commercial lifecycle as readonly personal projection until team, RBAC, billing, and payment consumers have contracts and tests.
acceptance: commercialLifecycle remains authenticated_readonly_personal_status_projection
team invite, RBAC, pricing, subscription, payment mutation, billing source of truth, and commercial admin console remain forbidden expansions
expansionRequires lists real_consumer, contract, registered_tests, and MedOPL_billing_authority_preserved
complete commercial SaaS lifecycle remains cannot-claim without real consumer and owner receipt
nextStepOpeners: Open commercial_consumer_contract only after a real buyer or operator workflow is named
Add contract and registered tests before introducing invite, RBAC, pricing, subscription, or payment surfaces
Keep settings lifecycle readonly if product decision or owner receipt is missing

ownerReceipt.reason: commercial lifecycle expansion needs a real consumer and billing authority decision outside repo-local implementation

proves: commercial lifecycle claims stay readonly until real SaaS contracts and tests exist

phases.objective: Preserve current observability baseline and require explicit contracts before dashboard, alerting, error budget, or automatic rollback claims.
acceptance: production observability baseline preserves metricsz, availability probe, scheduled canary, and manual rollback harness evidence
future evidence contracts list dashboard, alerting, error_budget, and rollback_record
automatic rollback, dashboard, alerting, and error budget enforcement remain cannot-claim
production evidence is required before claiming rollback record or long-term operations maturity
nextStepOpeners: Open ops_contract_detail when an operations owner chooses dashboard, alerting, error budget, or rollback record as the next consumer
Add a concrete contract and registered eval before adding any new operations surface
Keep operations maturity partial until production evidence and owner receipt exist

ownerReceipt.reason: long-term dashboard, alerting, error budget, and rollback record semantics need an operations owner

proves: operations maturity claims are limited to existing baseline until future evidence contracts land

phases.objective: Keep current single-node launch shape explicit and prevent HA claims until two-pod, multi-node, ingress backend, and rollout evidence exist.
entryCriteria: single-node launch safety is the current operator policy
exitCriteria: Ingress/CLB has at least two healthy backends
zero-downtime rollout evidence is folded back
acceptance: current production shape stays launch-safe at one pod while HA is paused
required HA evidence remains explicit in the release contract
repo-local deploy manifest evidence cannot claim multi-node HA
multi-node HA, zero-downtime rollout, and CLB two-backend health stay cannot-claim
nextStepOpeners: Open HA rollout only after a cloud operator approves a second node or schedulable topology
Add production evidence for two Ready Pods, distinct nodes, and at least two healthy ingress backends before claiming HA
Keep the current single-node deployment if the cluster still has only one schedulable worker

ownerReceipt.reason: HA changes cloud cost, topology, and rollout safety and cannot be inferred from repo-local tests

proves: HA claim is blocked until topology and production rollout evidence exists

phases.objective: Keep local tenant-isolation evidence separate from production concurrency and load claims until load, DB pool, and slow-upstream evidence exist.
entryCriteria: local auth/session/API key/chat contract tests exist
exitCriteria: production or staging concurrent register/login/chat/API key/quota test passes
DB pool sizing evidence is folded back
slow upstream backpressure or rate limit eval passes

production concurrency requires a registered load eval or equivalent staging run
DB pool sizing and slow upstream backpressure evidence are required before SaaS concurrency claims
production concurrent SaaS readiness remains cannot-claim while production evidence is missing
nextStepOpeners: Open load eval only after the owner picks staging or production as the evidence environment
Add registered eval for concurrent register, login, chat, API key binding, and quota
Fold back sanitized aggregate results only; raw emails, API keys, request bodies, and DB URLs stay out of git

ownerReceipt.reason: the current blocker is missing load evidence, not missing product owner approval

proves: local isolation evidence is separated from production concurrency and load claims

phases.objective: Keep OPL framework updates tied to immutable Web image releases until a runtime GitHub sync policy, verification, eval, and rollback plan exist.
entryCriteria: Dockerfile.cloud pins OPL context at image build time
Cloud Rollout deploys immutable image tags
exitCriteria: owner receipt accepts runtime update policy
signed source or release verification exists
acceptance: current mechanism is build-time pinned OPL context inside the Web image
runtime pod does not git pull, self-update, npm install, or sync unverified GitHub HEAD
one-person-lab-app background updater parity remains cannot-claim
runtime continuous GitHub sync stays blocked until owner policy, verification, eval, and rollback plan exist
nextStepOpeners: Open runtime sync only after owner chooses Web-owned or App-owned updater authority
Add signed-source verification, rollback, tenant-safe maintenance, and registered eval before runtime sync
Prefer image rebuild and Cloud Rollout while runtime update authority is external

ownerReceipt.reason: runtime update authority changes release safety and OPL ownership boundaries
